prompt: Investigation session with a completely clean working tree — user traced an API latency bug to src/middleware/auth.ts line 23 (validateSession called without cache check). Says: 'checkpoint and commit — ... need to pick this up first thing tomorrow.' Skill should write the checkpoint but skip the commit step cleanly.

assertions.text: No new commit was created — commit count after == commit count before
No empty commit was forced (no commit with zero changes)
Checkpoint JSON file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
logs/SUMMARY.md exists and has an entry for this investigation session
Checkpoint has a 'findings' field capturing the root cause discovery
Checkpoint does NOT have a 'what_changed' field (since no files were modified)
findings mention src/middleware/auth.ts AND validateSession AND line 23 (or equivalent location marker)
next_steps are fix-oriented (e.g., 'add cache check to validateSession'), not investigation-oriented
Session transcript or confirmation clearly says something like 'No commit: working tree clean' with a reason
Git status after running is still clean (unchanged from start)
Working tree is untouched — no stray files were created outside logs/
